2026-04-07T03:25:22.3603320Z   
2026-04-07T03:25:22.3603684Z   Now let me assess what documentation gaps exist:
2026-04-07T03:25:22.3604213Z   
2026-04-07T03:25:22.3604499Z   
2026-04-07T03:25:22.3610940Z   There are significant gaps in the documentation. The "Authenticate the Client" and "Next steps" sections are completely empty, and while the README covers the Service Health Alert example with ActionGroup and ActivityLogAlert, it's missing documentation for most of the other resource types the package supports—AlertRule, AutoscaleSetting, DataCollectionEndpoint, DataCollectionRule, DataCollectionRuleAssociation, and several others aren't documented at all. I'm noticing these gaps are pretty significant for a beta release—the README has implementation code but lacks critical documentation sections and examples for the most commonly used resources. I should probably file a GitHub issue about this and then check how comparable Provisioning packages structure their documentation to understand what the expected format should be.
2026-04-07T03:25:22.3616004Z

2026-04-07T03:25:22.3626176Z      
2026-04-07T03:25:22.3627324Z   The key concepts section doesn't explain the specific resource types available or when to use them, and the examples only demonstrate one scenario when the package actually supports 15+ resource types. 2026-04-07T03:25:22.3628711Z   
2026-04-07T03:25:22.3630309Z   Since provisioning libraries generate templates rather than authenticate directly, I need to clarify that authentication happens through `azd` or `Azure.ResourceManager` during deployment. I'll also need to add examples for commonly-used resources like DiagnosticSetting and MetricAlert, expand the next steps section with relevant Azure Monitor documentation, and document the specific resource types in the key concepts.

Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. 2026-04-07T03:25:22.5598143Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T03:23:30.564162651Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"# Azure Provisioning Monitor client library for .NET\n\nAzure.Provisioning.Monitor simplifies declarative resource provisioning in .NET.\n\n## Getting started\n\n### Install the package\n\nInstall the client library for .NET with [NuGet](https://www.nuget.org/ ):\n\n```dotnetcli\ndotnet add package Azure.Provisioning.Monitor --prerelease\n```\n\n### Prerequisites\n\n\u003e You must have an [Azure subscription](https://azure.microsoft.com/free/dotnet/).\n\n### Authenticate the Client\n\n## Key concepts\n\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using dotnet.  You can then use azd to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates.\n\n## Examples\n\n### Create a Service Health Alert with Action Group\n\nThis example demonstrates how to create an Azure service health alert that sends email notifications using an action group.\n\n```C# Snippet:MonitorServiceHealthAlert\nInfrastructure infra = new();\n\nProvisioningParameter alertName =\n    new(nameof(alertName), typeof(string))\n    {\n        Description = \"Specify the name of the alert.\"\n    };\ninfra.Add(alertName);\n\nProvisioningParameter emailAddress =\n    new(nameof(emailAddress), typeof(string))\n    {\n        Value = \"email@example.com\",\n        Description = \"Specify the email address where the alerts are sent to.\"\n    };\ninfra.Add(emailAddress);\n\nProvisioningParameter emailName =\n    new(nameof(emailName), typeof(string))\n    {\n        Value = \"Example\",\n        Description = \"Specify the email address name where the alerts are sent to.\"\n    };\ninfra.Add(emailName);\n\nActionGroup emailActionGroup =\n    new(nameof(emailActionGroup), ActionGroup.ResourceVersions.V2023_01_01)\n    {\n        Location = new AzureLocation(\"global\"),\n        GroupShortName = \"string\",\n        IsEnabled = true,\n        EmailReceivers =\n        {\n            new MonitorEmailReceiver\n            {\n                Name = emailName,\n                EmailAddress = emailAddress,\n                UseCommonAlertSchema = true\n            }\n        }\n    };\ninfra.Add(emailActionGroup);\n\nActivityLogAlert alert =\n    new(nameof(alert), ActivityLogAlert.ResourceVersions.V2020_10_01)\n    {\n        Name = alertName,\n        Location = new AzureLocation(\"global\"),\n        IsEnabled = true,\n        Scopes =\n        {\n            BicepFunction.GetSubscription().Id\n        },\n        ConditionAllOf =\n        {\n            new ActivityLogAlertAnyOfOrLeafCondition\n            {\n                Field = \"category\",\n                EqualsValue = \"ResourceHealth\"\n            }\n        },\n        ActionsActionGroups =\n        {\n            new ActivityLogAlertActionGroup\n            {\n                ActionGroupId = emailActionGroup.Id\n            }\n        }\n    };\ninfra.Add(alert);\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n-   File an issue via [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues).\n-   Check [previous questions](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/azure+.net) or ask new ones on Stack Overflow using Azure and .NET tags.\n\n## Next steps\n\n## Contributing\n\nFor details on contributing to this repository, see the [contributing\nguide][cg].\n\nThis project welcomes contributions and suggestions. Most contributions\nrequire you to agree to a Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring\nthat you have the right to, and actually do, grant us the rights to use\nyour contribution. Follow the instructions provided by the\nbot. You'll only need to do this action once across all repositories\nusing our CLA.\n\nThis project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct][coc].
